q:
It is dusk inside a built-up area.
Before the driver of the white car started overtaking, he briefly flashed the high beams.
Was he allowed to do that?

A. Yes.
B. No, because he is driving inside a built-up area.
e:
LESSON 11
When may you use the horn?
During the day:
Inside and outside built-up areas: to warn others and prevent an accident.
Outside built-up areas: to warn a driver you intend to overtake.
Between dusk and dawn:
Only in case of imminent danger.
In all other cases, instead of using the horn, you should flash the headlights (switch between high and low beams).
clean_q:
It is dusk inside a built-up area.
Before the driver of the white car started overtaking, he briefly flashed the high beams.
Was he allowed to do that?
A. Yes.
B. No, because he is driving inside a built-up area.
clean_e:
LESSON 11
When may you use the horn?
During the day:
Inside and outside built-up areas: to warn others and prevent an accident.
Outside built-up areas: to warn a driver you intend to overtake.
Between dusk and dawn:
Only in case of imminent danger.
In all other cases, instead of using the horn, you should flash the headlights (switch between high and low beams).

q:
There is heavy fog.
Should the front fog lights burn?
e:
LESSON 11- 'The lights’
The front fog lamps are not required on a car.
You are therefore never required to make them burn.
clean_q:
There is heavy fog.
Should the front fog lights burn?
clean_e:
LESSON 11- 'The lights’
The front fog lamps are not required on a car.
You are therefore never required to make them burn.

q: You must switch off the headlights (main beam) when the driver in front of you is less than (…) m away.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
50 meters.
clean_q: You must switch off the headlights (main beam) when the driver in front of you is less than (…) m away.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’. 
50 meters.

q:
There is a lot of smoke in a tunnel and you stop.
What do you do wrong?

A. You leave the tunnel through an emergency exit.
B. You leave the key in the ignition.
C. You lock the car.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
During heavy smoke or fire, you leave the tunnel through an emergency exit.
You don’t lock the doors of the car and you leave the key in the ignition.
clean_q:
There is a lot of smoke in a tunnel and you stop. 
What do you do wrong?
 
A. You leave the tunnel through an emergency exit. 
B. You leave the key in the ignition. 
C. You lock the car.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
During heavy smoke or fire, you leave the tunnel through an emergency exit. 
You don’t lock the doors of the car and you leave the key in the ignition.

q:
When is a driver, when it is fairly dark outside built-up areas, allowed to keep the high beams on?

A. When an oncoming vehicle is approaching.
B. When another car is driving in front of him.
C. When overtaking the car in front of him.
e:
LESSON 11
Article 30/1
High beams must be switched off and replaced by low beams:
a) when approaching an oncoming road user,
b) when approaching a rail vehicle or a boat whose driver could be dazzled,
c) when following another vehicle at less than 50 meters, except when overtaking,
d) when the road is continuously and sufficiently lit so the driver can clearly see up to about 100 meters.
clean_q:
When is a driver, when it is fairly dark outside built-up areas, allowed to keep the high beams on?
A. When an oncoming vehicle is approaching.
B. When another car is driving in front of him.
C. When overtaking the car in front of him.
clean_e:
LESSON 11
Article 30/1
High beams must be switched off and replaced by low beams:
a) when approaching an oncoming road user,
b) when approaching a rail vehicle or a boat whose driver could be dazzled,
c) when following another vehicle at less than 50 meters, except when overtaking,
d) when the road is continuously and sufficiently lit so the driver can clearly see up to about 100 meters.

q:
May you use the horn at night in case of imminent danger?

A. Yes, you may.
B. No, at night you should rather flash your high beams.
e:
LESSON 11
Only in case of imminent danger, such as when a car suddenly changes lanes, you may use the horn at night.
clean_q:
May you use the horn at night in case of imminent danger?
A. Yes, you may.
B. No, at night you should rather flash your high beams.
clean_e:
LESSON 11
Only in case of imminent danger, such as when a car suddenly changes lanes, you may use the horn at night.

q:
It’s snowing. When must you switch on your dipped-beam headlights?

A. Always when it’s snowing.
B. As soon as visibility is less than 100 metres.
C. As soon as visibility is less than 200 metres.
e:
LESSON 11
During the day, you must switch on your dipped-beam headlights:
- in rain, when you cannot see farther than 200 metres;
- in fog, when you cannot see farther than 200 metres;
- in snowfall, when you cannot see farther than 200 metres;
- or in other situations (e.g. smoke from a fire) when visibility is less than 200 metres.
clean_q:
It’s snowing. When must you switch on your dipped-beam headlights?
A. Always when it’s snowing.
B. As soon as visibility is less than 100 metres.
C. As soon as visibility is less than 200 metres.

q:
It is night, outside a built-up area.
While the blue car is overtaking, the distracted driver of the white car suddenly swerves to the left.
What may the driver of the blue car do?

A. He may only use the main beams because it is night.
B. He may use the horn to avoid an accident.
e:
LESSON 11
When may you use the horn?
During the day:
Inside and outside built-up areas: to warn others and prevent an accident.
Outside built-up areas: to warn a driver you intend to overtake.
Between dusk and dawn:
Only in case of imminent danger.
In all other cases, you must flash your headlights alternately instead of using the horn.

It is night, outside a built-up area.

A. He may only use the main beams because it is night.
B. He may use the horn to avoid an accident.
clean_e:
LESSON 11
When may you use the horn?
During the day:
Inside and outside built-up areas: to warn others and prevent an accident.
Outside built-up areas: to warn a driver you intend to overtake.
Between dusk and dawn:
Only in case of imminent danger.
In all other cases, you must flash your headlights alternately instead of using the horn.

q: Is it allowed to turn on the four warning lights to alert the driver behind you that there is a traffic jam?
e:
LESSON 11- 'The lights’.
You may only use the four warning lights for signaling:
. a school bus with children during embarkation or disembarkation;
. a vehicle that is defective or immobilized after an accident;
. a dangerous situation as it suddenly slowing the vehicles before you.
clean_q: Is it allowed to turn on the four warning lights to alert the driver behind you that there is a traffic jam?

q:
Your car is parked.
When is the rear fog lamp allowed to be used?

A. Only in fog.
B. Only in fog and snow.
C. In fog, snow and heavy rain.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
When you park your car, the rear fog lamp may burn in case of fog, snowfall or heavy rain.
It is not an obligation.
clean_q:
Your car is parked. 
When is the rear fog lamp allowed to be used? 
A. Only in fog. 
B. Only in fog and snow. 
C. In fog, snow and heavy rain.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
When you park your car, the rear fog lamp may burn in case of fog, snowfall or heavy rain. 
It is not an obligation.

q:
Is it allowed to give a warning signal with the horn of your car when you want to overtake another driver?

A. Yes, during the day outside built-up areas.
B. Yes, during the day in built-up areas.
C. Yes, always in built-up areas.
e:
LESSON 11- 'The lights’
Horn during the day:
. Inside and outside built-up areas: to warn others and thus to avoid an accident.
. Outside built-up areas: to warn a driver that you want to catch up.
clean_q:
Is it allowed to give a warning signal with the horn of your car when you want to overtake another driver?
A. Yes, during the day outside built-up areas.
B. Yes, during the day in built-up areas.
C. Yes, always in built-up areas.
clean_e:
LESSON 11- 'The lights’
Horn during the day:
. Inside and outside built-up areas: to warn others and thus to avoid an accident.
. Outside built-up areas: to warn a driver that you want to catch up.

q:
When must the rear fog lights be used during heavy rainfall?

A. The visibility is less than 200 meters.
B. The visibility is less than 100 meters.
C. Always.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
Due to fog or snowfall, whenever the visibility is reduced to less than 100 meters.
Due to heavy rainfall, always in Belgium.

C. Always.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
Due to fog or snowfall, whenever the visibility is reduced to less than 100 meters.
Due to heavy rainfall, always in Belgium.

A. Correct.

e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
If you do so, then you make a double violation.

q:
Which lights are always used together with the dipped beam headlights (low beam)?
1. Two red rear lights
2. Two parking lights
3. Two front fog lights

A. Only 1.
B. Only 1 and 2.
C. 1, 2 and 3.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
Together with the dipped beam headlights, two red rear lights and two position lights are on.

A. The front and rear fog lamps.
B. Only the front fog lamps.
C. Only the rear fog lamps.

The front fog lamps are not required on a car.

q:
Nighttime, outside a built-up area.
Before starting to overtake, the driver of the white car briefly used the horn.
Was he allowed to do that?

A. No, because it is night.
B. Yes, because it is outside the built-up area.
e:
LESSON 11
When may you use the horn?
- During the day:
Inside and outside built-up areas: to warn others and prevent an accident.
Outside built-up areas: to warn a driver you intend to overtake.
- Between dusk and dawn:
Only in case of imminent danger.
In all other cases, you must flash your headlights alternately instead of using the horn.

clean_e:
LESSON 11
When may you use the horn?
- During the day:
Inside and outside built-up areas: to warn others and prevent an accident.
Outside built-up areas: to warn a driver you intend to overtake.
- Between dusk and dawn:
Only in case of imminent danger.
In all other cases, you must flash your headlights alternately instead of using the horn.

q:
There is an accident without casualties.
Are you allowed to leave the four hazard warning lights on?
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
The four hazard lights of a car can only be switched on when:
. your car has broken down.
. in order to warn other road users of a real danger.
. a school bus when pupils are getting in or out.
clean_q:
There is an accident without casualties.
Are you allowed to leave the four hazard warning lights on?
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
The four hazard lights of a car can only be switched on when:
. your car has broken down.
. in order to warn other road users of a real danger.
. a school bus when pupils are getting in or out.

q:
How do you react when you drive into a fogbank?

A. You brake firmly.
B. You slow down gently.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
You should definitely not suddenly brake.
clean_q:
How do you react when you drive into a fogbank? 
A. You brake firmly. 
B. You slow down gently.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
You should definitely not suddenly brake.

q:
A school bus has stopped and the hazard warning lights are being used. What do you have to do?

A. Stop.
B. Slow down and stop if necessary.
C. Drive on.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
If a school bus stops so that students can go out, the driver has to switch on the four direction indicators or hazard warning lights.
Upcoming vehicles are obliged to reduce speed and if it is necessary to stop
They should be careful while driving by the bus.
clean_q:
A school bus has stopped and the hazard warning lights are being used. What do you have to do? 
A. Stop.
B. Slow down and stop if necessary. 
C. Drive on.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
If a school bus stops so that students can go out, the driver has to switch on the four direction indicators or hazard warning lights.
Upcoming vehicles are obliged to reduce speed and if it is necessary to stop
They should be careful while driving by the bus.

q:
There is fog and visibility is only 90 meters.
The white car is parked.
Which lights must be switched on?

A. The parking lights at the front, and at the rear: one or two red lights.
B. The parking lights and the dipped-beam headlights.
C. The parking lights, dipped-beam headlights and the rear fog light.
e:
LESSON 11
When you are stationary or parked on the road or on the verge: between sunset and sunrise; and whenever it is no longer possible to see clearly for about 200 meters, the following lights must be on:
at the front: one or two white or yellow parking lights
at the rear: one or two red lights.
In foggy weather, snowfall, or heavy rain, the dipped headlights or front fog lights and also the rear fog lights may be used, but it is not required.
clean_q:
There is fog and visibility is only 90 meters.
The white car is parked.
Which lights must be switched on?
A. The parking lights at the front, and at the rear: one or two red lights.
B. The parking lights and the dipped-beam headlights.
C. The parking lights, dipped-beam headlights and the rear fog light.
clean_e:
LESSON 11
When you are stationary or parked on the road or on the verge: between sunset and sunrise; and whenever it is no longer possible to see clearly for about 200 meters, the following lights must be on:
at the front: one or two white or yellow parking lights
at the rear: one or two red lights.
In foggy weather, snowfall, or heavy rain, the dipped headlights or front fog lights and also the rear fog lights may be used, but it is not required.

q:
Which lights must be switched on when you drive in a tunnel and you can’t see 200 meters ahead?

A. Lights are not obligatory.
B. The fog lights.
C. The dipped beam headlights.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
If you cannot see 200 meters far, then switch the dipped beam headlights lamps on before you drive in the tunnel, so that the driver behind you doesn't think that you brake at the entrance of the tunnel.
clean_q:
Which lights must be switched on when you drive in a tunnel and you can’t see 200 meters ahead? 
A. Lights are not obligatory. 
B. The fog lights. 
C. The dipped beam headlights.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
If you cannot see 200 meters far, then switch the dipped beam headlights lamps on before you drive in the tunnel, so that the driver behind you doesn't think that you brake at the entrance of the tunnel.

q:
On this road within the built up area the headlights can be used instead of the dipped beam headlights.

A. Correct.
B. Incorrect.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
It should not, because the roadway is adequately illuminated, so you easily can see 100 meters far.
clean_q:
On this road within the built up area the headlights can be used instead of the dipped beam headlights. 
A. Correct.
B. Incorrect.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.

q:
You park your car on an unlit road in the evening. What lights must be lit?
1. The parking lights.
2. The two red rear lights.
3. The dipped beam headlights (low beam).

A. Only 1.
B. 1 and 2.
C. 1, 2 and 3.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
At the front: one or two white or yellow sidelights.
At the back: one or two red lights.
clean_q:
You park your car on an unlit road in the evening. What lights must be lit?

A. Only 1.
B. 1 and 2.
C. 1, 2 and 3.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
At the front: one or two white or yellow sidelights.
At the back: one or two red lights.

q:
There is heavy rain and visibility is reduced to 130 metres. Must the rear fog lights be switched on?

A. Yes, always in heavy rain.
B. No, because visibility is more than 100 metres.
C. Rear fog lights are never compulsory.
e:
LESSON 11
The rear fog light must be switched on:
in fog, when visibility is less than about 100 metres;
in snowfall, when visibility is less than about 100 metres;
in heavy rain, it must always be on in Belgium, regardless of visibility.
clean_q:
There is heavy rain and visibility is reduced to 130 metres. Must the rear fog lights be switched on?
A. Yes, always in heavy rain. 
B. No, because visibility is more than 100 metres.
C. Rear fog lights are never compulsory.

q:
You park your car on the road or on the verge during nightfall.
Are you allowed to leave the dipped beam headlights on?
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
They may continue to burn. But the position lights are sufficient.
clean_q:
You park your car on the road or on the verge during nightfall.
Are you allowed to leave the dipped beam headlights on?
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
They may continue to burn. But the position lights are sufficient.

q:
It is night. A driver is overtaking another car.
May the high beams be on during the overtaking?

A. Yes.
B. No.
e:
LESSON 11
High beams must not be used:
- when the road is continuously and sufficiently lit so that you can see 100 metres ahead;
- when they would dazzle an oncoming road user;
- when a vehicle is driving less than 50 metres ahead of you and you are not overtaking it immediately. If you are overtaking, you may use them.
- when approaching a rail vehicle or a boat.
clean_q:
It is night. A driver is overtaking another car.
May the high beams be on during the overtaking?
A. Yes. 
B. No.

q:
From when must the front fog light be switched on in heavy fog?

A. When visibility is less than 200 metres.
B. When visibility is less than 100 metres.
C. Never.
e:
LESSON 11
A car is not required to have front fog lights.
Therefore, there is no traffic rule that obliges drivers to use them.
clean_q:
From when must the front fog light be switched on in heavy fog?
A. When visibility is less than 200 metres.
B. When visibility is less than 100 metres.
C. Never.
clean_e:
LESSON 11
A car is not required to have front fog lights.
Therefore, there is no traffic rule that obliges drivers to use them.

LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
100 meters.
clean_q: When it’s snowing, the rear fog lights must be used when the visibility is less than (…) meters.
clean_e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
100 meters.

q:
There is a thick fog and you park the car on the road.
What’s wrong?

A. The rear fog light may be on.
B. The parking lights and the rear fog light must be lit.
C. The front fog lights may be on.
e:
LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
The dipped headlights or front fog-lamps don’t have to burn. But it is allowed.

LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
The dipped headlights or front fog-lamps don’t have to burn. But it is allowed.

A. Correct.
B. Incorrect.

LESSON 11 – ‘The lights’.
They may burn together with the dipped beam headlights or without.
